body.title: CLOSED: Backfill ticket status prefixes in titles + created_at dates from GitHub
body.result: status prefixes embedded in title on every status change; backfill-prefixes applied to 22 open tickets; backfill-dates populated 272 created_at from GitHub
body.description:
Two ticket hygiene gaps making queue unworkable:

1. **Status not in title** — searching the DB or GitHub for 'sprint tickets' requires querying the status JSON field. Title must embed the status so grep works: `[sprint] Swarm web tab UI`. When status changes, update the title prefix. This makes `cc_queue list | grep sprint` and GitHub issue search both work in one pass.

2. **created_at missing on ~1000 tickets** — can't do 'tickets filed this week' queries. Fix: for each ticket with null created_at that has a github_issue number, fetch the GitHub issue creation date via `gh api`. Write back to metadata.

**Affected files:** TheIgors/lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (add title-prefix sync on every status change + add a backfill command `cc_queue.py backfill-dates`)

**Design rules:** no-sqlite; docs-in-code

**Scope boundary:** IN — add `[STATUS]` prefix to title on every cmd_done/cmd_claim/cmd_setstatus/cmd_block; `cc_queue.py backfill-dates` fetches GH issue created_at for all null-date tickets; strip old DESIGNED:/SPRINT:/etc prefixes before adding new ones. OUT — changing the status emoji display, changing underlying status field.

**Test plan:** test_cc_queue_status_prefix.py — setstatus updates title prefix; backfill-dates populates created_at from GH mock; listing output is grep-able by status.

**Pass condition:** `cc_queue.py list | grep -c '\[sprint\]'` returns the correct count; `cc_queue.py show T-swarm-web-tab-ui | jq .created_at` returns a non-null ISO date; `cc_queue.py backfill-dates --dry-run` prints N dates to fill without erroring.
